disclosureBanner.announce: Privacy notice available. A shield button in the top-left opens the details.
disclosureBanner.text: This app reports anonymous usage events to help us keep it healthy. No account, no tracking cookies, no third-party analytics. You can change this any time.
docent.category.none: I didn't find datasets in the "{category}" category. Try a different topic or search term.
docent.explain.noDataset: You're looking at Earth with real-time cloud cover. Browse the datasets or ask me about a topic — I'll load something onto the globe for you.
docent.greeting.1: Welcome to Science on a Sphere! I'm Orbit, your digital docent — ask me about any topic and I'll find a dataset to show you. Try asking about oceans, climate, hurricanes, or anything else that interests you.
docent.greeting.2: Hello! I'm here to guide you through over 500 visualizations of our planet and beyond. What would you like to explore? You can ask about a topic, or I can tell you more about whatever's on the globe right now.
docent.greeting.3: Hi there! Think of me as your personal guide to Earth science data. Ask me about weather, the ocean floor, space, volcanoes — or just say "show me something interesting" and I'll pick something out.
docent.help:
Here's how I can help:

• **Ask about a topic** — "Tell me about hurricanes" or "Show me ocean temperatures"
• **Explore categories** — "Show me atmosphere datasets" or "What about space?"
• **Learn about what's showing** — "Explain this" or "What am I looking at?"
• **Find related data** — "Show me something similar" or "More like this"
• **Search freely** — Just type any question and I'll find relevant datasets
docent.patterns.explain: explain, explain this, explain it, tell me about this, tell me about it, tell me about the current, tell me about what's showing, tell me more about this, tell me more about it, describe this, describe it, describe the current, what does this show, what does this mean
docent.patterns.greeting: hi, hello, hey, howdy, greetings, good morning, good afternoon, good evening, sup, yo
docent.patterns.help: help, what can you do, how does this work, how do this work, how does it work, how do it work, commands, options
docent.patterns.related: show me something similar, show me something related, show me similar, show me related, show me like this, related, more like this, similar
docent.patterns.whatIs: what's this, what is this, where am i, what am i looking at, what am i seeing
docent.related.noDataset: There's no dataset loaded right now. Ask me about a topic and I'll find something to show you!
docent.related.none: I couldn't find datasets closely related to "{title}". Try asking about a specific topic instead.
docent.search.fallbackResults: I didn't find an exact match for "{query}", but here are some results for "{word}":
docent.search.none: I couldn't find datasets matching "{query}". Try different keywords, or ask me about a broad topic like "ocean", "climate", or "space".
docent.search.singleMatch:
I found a dataset that matches: **{title}**

{desc}{qa}
docent.system.respondInLanguage: Respond in {language}. Even when the user writes in English, your reply, dataset summaries, and any captions must be in {language}. Keep <<LOAD:DATASET_ID>> markers and tool-call syntax exactly as specified.
docent.system.respondInLanguageReminder: REMINDER: Reply only in {language} — even if the user typed in another language. Keep <<LOAD:...>> markers and tool-call syntax literal.

help.guide.section.downloads:
Offline downloads

  • Click the download icon on any dataset card to cache it locally.
  • Downloaded datasets open instantly, even without an internet connection.
  • Manage cached datasets from the download panel to see sizes or delete old ones.
help.guide.section.exploring:
Exploring datasets

  • Tap the Browse button in the bottom-right to open the dataset list.
  • Filter by category or search by keyword to narrow the list.
  • Click any card to load that dataset onto the globe.
  • The home button (top-left) returns you to the default Earth view and reopens browse.
help.guide.section.multiglobe:
Multi-globe comparisons

  • In a multi-panel layout, camera motion syncs across every globe — drag any panel to rotate them all.
  • Each panel has a small numbered button in its top-left corner. Tap one to make it the active (primary) panel.
  • The next dataset you load goes into the active panel. Use the Browse button to pick another dataset for the next panel.
  • Time-series animations sync by real-world date, not video seconds — two datasets with different date ranges still line up correctly during any overlap. A panel whose dataset doesn't cover the current time is dimmed and marked "No data for current time".
help.guide.section.navigating:
Navigating the globe

  • Drag to rotate the Earth.
  • Scroll or pinch to zoom.
  • Double-click a location to focus on it.
help.guide.section.orbit:
Talking to Orbit

  • Orbit is the digital docent — an AI assistant that can recommend datasets and explain what you're seeing.
  • Open the chat panel from the bottom-left trigger or the speech-bubble button in the browse panel.
  • Orbit can embed load buttons directly in its replies — click one to jump straight to that dataset.
  • Use the thumbs-up / thumbs-down buttons under each reply to rate Orbit's answers.
  • Configure the AI endpoint, model, and reading level from Tools → Orbit settings.
help.guide.section.privacy:
Privacy

The app reports anonymous usage events — which layers load, how long they're viewed, whether anything errored. There is no account, no tracking cookie, and no third-party analytics service. Feedback you submit is stored with your browser's user agent and the page URL; attaching a screenshot is opt-in.

You can switch between Essential, Research, and Off under Tools → Privacy; the change takes effect immediately. Read the full privacy policy →
help.guide.section.shortcuts:
Keyboard shortcuts

  • Esc — close the active panel
  • Enter — send a chat message
  • Space — toggle playback when a video dataset is loaded
help.guide.section.tools:
Tools & settings

  • The wrench icon next to Browse opens the Tools popover.
  • View — toggle labels, borders, 3D terrain, auto-rotation, the dataset info panel, and dataset legends.
  • Dataset info — show or hide the dataset metadata panel in the bottom-left. Hidden by default if you've turned it off previously; your preference is remembered.
  • Legend — show or hide the legend for each loaded dataset. In multi-globe layouts each panel shows its own legend; tap a legend to enlarge.
  • Layout — switch between a single globe or 2–4 synchronised globes that move together but can show different datasets side-by-side.
  • Clear — remove any markers or highlighted regions.
  • Orbit settings — configure the AI docent's endpoint, model, and preferences.
  • Tap outside the popover or press Esc to close it.
help.guide.section.tours:
Guided tours

  • Tours are scripted sequences that fly the camera, swap datasets, and narrate a topic.
  • Start a tour from the browse panel — tour cards are marked with a play icon.
  • Use the on-screen transport controls to play, pause, or skip between tour steps.
  • Some tours pause for questions or user input; click continue to resume.

publisher.datasetDetail.action.confirm.r: Retract this dataset? It will be hidden from the public catalog. You can re-publish later.

publisher.datasetDetail.action.error.ser: The server returned an error ({detail}). Check the deployment's bindings, then try again.

Can't publish — the server returned these validation errors:
publisher.datasetDetail.action.publishDi: Waiting on transcode to finish before this can be published.
publisher.datasetDetail.notFound: Dataset not found. It may have been deleted, or you may not have access.
publisher.datasetDetail.preview.body: This link opens the draft as a live globe rendering for the next {minutes} minutes. Anyone with the link can view the draft until it expires.
publisher.datasetDetail.preview.heading: Preview link ready
publisher.datasetForm.deferredHint: Recommended fields (abstract, organization, license, time range, categories, keywords) ship in sub-phase 3pc/C. Drafts can be saved with the required fields only.
publisher.datasetForm.field.title: Title
publisher.datasetForm.help.abstract: Markdown supported. Headings (##, ###), **bold**, *italic*, lists, code, blockquotes, links.
publisher.datasetForm.help.boundingBox: Optional. The data's geographic extent — fill all four corners to clip the data to a region over a base Earth; leave blank for a full-globe (equirectangular) dataset. North ≥ South; N/S in [-90, 90], W/E in [-180, 180].
publisher.datasetForm.help.celestialBody: Optional. The body the data visualises (Earth, Mars, Moon, Sun…). Blank means Earth.
publisher.datasetForm.help.dataRef: Required to publish (drafts may leave it blank). Accepts a Vimeo ID (`vimeo:1234567`), an R2 path (`r2:videos/.../master.m3u8` or `r2:datasets/.../image.png`), or an absolute HTTPS URL. A guided uploader replaces this in a later phase.
publisher.datasetForm.help.dataRefManual: Optional. Use this to swap the row to a legacy `vimeo:` / `url:` reference or paste an existing `r2:videos/.../master.m3u8` path without re-uploading bytes. The uploader above is the normal path for new content.
publisher.datasetForm.help.flippedInY: Check if the source imagery is authored with an inverted (top-to-bottom) Y axis.
publisher.datasetForm.help.geography: The globe expects equirectangular (plate carrée, EPSG:4326) imagery. Reproject other map projections first — e.g. `gdalwarp -t_srs EPSG:4326` — or render the data through a Zyra workflow.
publisher.datasetForm.help.keywords: Press Enter or comma to add. Max 20 keywords, 40 characters each.
publisher.datasetForm.help.legend: Optional. PNG, JPEG, or WebP — the colour-scale legend shown alongside the loaded dataset. Upload above (saved drafts), or paste an `r2:` / absolute HTTPS reference.
publisher.datasetForm.help.licenseSpdx: SPDX identifier (e.g., CC0-1.0, CC-BY-4.0). One of SPDX or License statement is required before publishing.
publisher.datasetForm.help.lonOrigin: Optional. Degrees offset for the longitude axis: 0 = prime-meridian centred, ±180 = dateline centred.
publisher.datasetForm.help.period: ISO 8601 duration: P1D (daily), PT1H (hourly), P1M (monthly), PT15M (every 15 minutes).
publisher.datasetForm.help.radiusMi: Optional. Radius of the celestial body in miles (pair with a non-Earth body for proportional sizing).
publisher.datasetForm.help.slug: Auto-derived from the title; edit to customise. Must start with a letter and use lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens only.
publisher.datasetForm.help.tags: Press Enter or comma to add. Free-form labels — use for what categories and keywords don't already cover.
publisher.datasetForm.help.thumbnail: Optional. PNG, JPEG, or WebP — the image shown on the browse card. Upload above (saved drafts), or paste an `r2:` / absolute HTTPS reference.
publisher.datasetForm.help.timeRange: Times entered in your local timezone are stored as UTC. Set both Start and End together (or neither); the publish-readiness check enforces start ≤ end.
publisher.datasetForm.placeholder.abstra: Monthly mean sea-surface temperature anomaly relative to the 1991-2020 climatology. Generated by Zyra workflow `sst-anomaly-monthly`.
publisher.datasetForm.placeholder.licens: Public domain / U.S. Government. May be reproduced without permission.
publisher.datasetForm.placeholder.title: Sea Surface Temperature Anomaly — April 2026
publisher.datasetForm.preview.empty: Nothing to preview yet. Switch back to Edit and write some markdown.
publisher.datasetForm.transcoding.notice: A video transcode is in progress for this row. Reload this page or open the dataset's detail view to see when it finishes — typically 1–10 minutes depending on the source length. Changes to the data reference can't be saved while a transcode is running.

publisher.feeds.intro: The feeds this node ingests current events from. Every ingested event lands in the review queue as proposed.
publisher.feeds.preset.cams: Air-quality and atmosphere monitoring news from Copernicus.
publisher.feeds.preset.copernicus: News from the EU's Earth-observation programme.
publisher.feeds.preset.eonet: Curated natural events (storms, wildfires, volcanoes) with location and time built in.
publisher.feeds.preset.gdacs: Global disaster alerts (floods, cyclones, quakes) with coordinates.
publisher.feeds.preset.gvp: Weekly volcanic-activity reports from the Global Volcanism Program.
publisher.feeds.preset.nasaEo: Stories and imagery about Earth from NASA's Earth Observatory.
publisher.feeds.preset.nhcAtlantic: Active Atlantic hurricane and tropical-storm advisories.
publisher.feeds.preset.nhcEpacific: Active East Pacific hurricane and tropical-storm advisories.
publisher.feeds.preset.noaaNews: NOAA's newsroom — oceans, weather, climate, fisheries.
publisher.feeds.preset.nwsSevere: Live severe and extreme weather alerts across the United States.
publisher.feeds.preset.nyt: The New York Times climate desk.
publisher.feeds.preset.ptwc: Pacific tsunami warnings and advisories, with coordinates.
publisher.feeds.preset.usgsQuakes: Significant earthquakes with precise time and coordinates.

publisher.nodeProfile.intro: Describe the organization hosting this node. AI-assisted drafts (blog posts, tours) ground themselves in this profile so they speak in your organization's voice.

publisher.nodeProfile.logo.error.type: The logo must be a PNG, JPEG, or WebP image.

publisher.tours.delete.confirm: Delete tour "{title}"? This can't be undone.

publisher.tours.empty.title: No tours yet.
publisher.tours.heading: Tours
publisher.tours.intro: Compose narrated sequences of camera positions and dataset loads. Click New tour to capture the first step.
publisher.tours.retract.confirm: Retract "{title}" from the public list? You can republish it later — the immutable snapshot stays in place.

publisher.workflows.col.name: Name
publisher.workflows.empty.hint: Create one to keep a real-time dataset updating itself on a schedule.
publisher.workflows.empty.title: No workflows yet.
publisher.workflows.error: Could not load workflows. Check your access and try again.
publisher.workflows.form.createTarget.fa: Could not create the draft dataset.
publisher.workflows.form.createTarget.ne: Enter a workflow name first — it becomes the dataset title.
publisher.workflows.form.name: Name
publisher.workflows.form.pipeline.hint: YAML or JSON. Stages are checked against the server allowlist; at least one stage must write /work/output/dataset.mp4.
publisher.workflows.form.pipelineParseEr: The pipeline is not valid YAML or JSON.
publisher.workflows.form.schedule.hint: ISO-8601 duration between PT15M and P90D — e.g. PT1H (hourly), P1D (daily), P1W (weekly).
publisher.workflows.form.target.hint: The dataset this workflow overwrites in place each run. Create the draft dataset first, then paste its id here.
publisher.workflows.form.template.applie: Template applied — edit the source URL, pattern, and metadata before saving.
publisher.workflows.form.template.hint: JSON object of dataset fields. String values may use {{run_date}}, {{run_id}}, {{data_start}}, {{data_end}}, {{data_period}} — set start_time / end_time / period so real-time freshness works.
publisher.workflows.form.template.overwr: Replace the current pipeline and metadata template with the selected template?
publisher.workflows.form.templateParseEr: The metadata template is not valid JSON.
publisher.workflows.heading: Workflows
publisher.workflows.intro: Scheduled Zyra pipelines that keep real-time datasets fresh. Each workflow renders new data on a cadence and re-publishes its target dataset in place.
publisher.workflows.runNow.inProgress: A run is already in progress.
